kw-simple-rename: looks good as general guidance - it asked for the stack and the rename procedure is right, though it assumed Unity/C# rather than Swift
kw-simple-test: this works - solid Jest suite with fake timers covering debounce coalescing, args, and cancel; tail truncated but the delivered tests are correct
kw-mod-settings: doesn't work as written - the Slider takes a value instead of a binding and NavigationBarTitle is misused as a view, so it won't compile
kw-mod-sleep: this works as a self-contained demo - the timer pauses playback correctly; truncated near the end but the core logic is all there
kw-cx-refactor: looks good - sensible decomposition into controller/state/inventory/combat/network/persistence with an orchestrator; truncated but the plan is complete enough to act on
pr-crit-truncate: this works - correctly diagnosed the non-atomic write race and the temp-file + os.replace + fsync fix is exactly right (minor LOCK_UN typo in the non-recommended path)
pr-cx-mixer: not usable for the ask - it invented a from-scratch C++ engine with a placeholder EQ instead of wiring into the existing playback path, and it's truncated mid-class
pr-cx-stall: this works - the three stall causes (zombie fetch state, no backoff, reconnect race) are exactly the failure modes, and the FSM rework addresses them; truncation only cost boilerplate
pr-mod-scrubber: not working as-is - the layout and CSS are right but the script is cut off before the time-update logic, so the delivered code is incomplete
pr-mod-eqpreset: this works - clean UserDefaults persistence pattern (plus the other platforms), complete and correct
pr-simple-tint: this works - the low-alpha warm overlay approach is right and shown for three stacks; only a trailing note got truncated
